message:
fix(bayesian_ledger): reject non-finite Evidence before it poisons log_posterior (ft-n4fdx)

Evidence variants OutputRate(f64), Entropy(f64), TimeSinceOutput(f64),
ScrollbackGrowth(f64) fed NaN or Inf values unchecked into
gaussian_log_likelihoods / time_since_output_log_likelihoods, which
return a `[f64; 7]` full of NaN. PaneClassifier::update then does
  log_posterior[i] += ll
and NaN+x=NaN, so a single bad observation permanently pins all 7
posteriors at NaN. Every subsequent classify() returns NaN
probabilities — the classifier is silently dead for that pane.

Fail-closed fix at BayesianClassifier::update:
  - Introduce Evidence::is_finite() (true for PatternDetection,
    otherwise forwards to f64::is_finite on the carried value).
  - update() early-returns if evidence is not finite. A NaN/Inf-only
    stream on an unseen pane_id does NOT create a PaneClassifier entry
    — unknown panes stay unknown.

Matches the shape of ft-b4l62 (Histogram::record), ft-yskcu
(continuous_backpressure), ft-0p5q5 (EValueMonitor::observe), ft-c17iw
(semantic_anomaly::{Semantic,Conformal}AnomalyDetector::observe),
ft-761tz (disk_pressure).

4 regression tests cover: NaN Evidence does not poison posterior,
+Inf/-Inf Evidence preserves finite posterior, NaN-only evidence on
unseen pane is a no-op, PatternDetection still flows through after
rejected NaN.

69 bayesian_ledger::tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
